title: rubus hirtus var. henryanus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. henryanus, also known as the Hawaiian raspberry or mountain raspberry, is a variety of the Rubus hirtus plant species, which belongs to the rose family and produces edible fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and is believed to have various health benefits such as reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and providing antimicrobial protection. Herbal preparations of Rubus hirtus var. henryanus include infusions, tinctures, and teas, which can be used to support overall health and well-being. Additionally, the plant's fruit and leaves can be used in cooking and as a natural remedy for digestive issues and wounds.
